title: Employment Verification Services | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP
content:
## Employment Verification Services

### Manage operational risk.
In today’s complex hiring environment—particularly in light of the COVID-19 pandemic —workforces are more geographically distributed, the employee onboarding process is challenging, and many employers have shifted to remote hiring practices.
Compliance with employment verification requirements in this changed landscape has become a moving target—but adhering to evolving laws and regulations has never been more important.
Employers who violate these laws are subject to significant civil fines and penalties, not to mention public relations issues and interruptions to day-to-day business operations in the event of an audit.
From audits, guidance and training, to advanced technology tools, Fragomen provides comprehensive employment verification services to help you minimize liability and manage your operational risk.

### Employment verification compliance fueled by experience.
Fragomen has been customizing employment verification solutions for decades.
Our experienced employment verification professionals understand country-specific requirements, nuances and best practices. They can help you implement and maintain a sound program—and avoid unforeseen risks.
Our employment verification services include:
- Sophisticated online software solutions Guidance on employment verification compliance and best practices Process, procedure and policy review and development Preventative employment verification audit and review Training for human resources professionals and teams Customized program design, management and support Representation in the event of government visits and investigations
- Sophisticated online software solutions Guidance on employment verification compliance and best practices Process, procedure and policy review and development Preventative employment verification audit and review Training for human resources professionals and teams Customized program design, management and support Representation in the event of government visits and investigations
- Sophisticated online software solutions
- Guidance on employment verification compliance and best practices
- Process, procedure and policy review and development
- Preventative employment verification audit and review
- Training for human resources professionals and teams
- Customized program design, management and support
- Representation in the event of government visits and investigations

### Greater organization and compliance supported by employer verification technology.
We have developed solutions for I-9 completion that combine unparalleled flexibility with the level of trust that you would expect from a leading law firm and the immediacy and responsiveness that the government requires.
Fragomen’s WorkRight US I-9 technology is a flexible, end-to-end Form I-9 software tool that helps companies address the complexities and challenges of today’s government compliance requirements. When employees have access to a modern, streamlined, simplified employment verification solution, employers increase their level of compliance and minimize their exposure to risk.
- I-9 completion, signature, secure storing and tracking Informative, configurable notifications throughout the employment verification process Audits and conversion of historical data Legal guidance and HelpDesk support Integration with E-Verify I-9 policy review and consulting Ongoing training and legal updates I-9 and E-Verify audit and compliance support by our Government Strategies and Compliance Group
- I-9 completion, signature, secure storing and tracking Informative, configurable notifications throughout the employment verification process Audits and conversion of historical data Legal guidance and HelpDesk support Integration with E-Verify I-9 policy review and consulting Ongoing training and legal updates I-9 and E-Verify audit and compliance support by our Government Strategies and Compliance Group
- I-9 completion, signature, secure storing and tracking
- Informative, configurable notifications throughout the employment verification process
- Audits and conversion of historical data
- Legal guidance and HelpDesk support
- Integration with E-Verify
- I-9 policy review and consulting
- Ongoing training and legal updates
- I-9 and E-Verify audit and compliance support by our Government Strategies and Compliance Group
We continuously modernize our WorkRight US I-9 technology because we take the employment verification process—and the employee experience—extremely seriously.
Fragomen’s WorkRight UK solution helps UK employers compliantly navigate the process of verifying an employee’s right to work, and applies to UK visa holders, British nationals and Irish nationals. WorkRight uses facial recognition and fraud-detection software to increase employers’ compliance and minimize exposure to risk. By incorporating it at the start of the hiring process or during the course of employment, this tool provides a simple and fast way for individuals to prove their right to work in the UK.
Key benefits include:
- Initiating single or multiple requests Deterring identity fraud through use of identification and facial recognition technology, while helping meet compliance obligations Creating consistency in your right-to-work checks, saving time and reducing risk Achieving process efficiencies that help minimize costs and administrative burden for employers Ability to perform checks consistent with the latest UK legislation Access to Fragomen’s wider immigration support teams when needed, to ensure employees transition seamlessly into the UK
- Initiating single or multiple requests Deterring identity fraud through use of identification and facial recognition technology, while helping meet compliance obligations Creating consistency in your right-to-work checks, saving time and reducing risk Achieving process efficiencies that help minimize costs and administrative burden for employers Ability to perform checks consistent with the latest UK legislation Access to Fragomen’s wider immigration support teams when needed, to ensure employees transition seamlessly into the UK
- Initiating single or multiple requests
- Deterring identity fraud through use of identification and facial recognition technology, while helping meet compliance obligations
- Creating consistency in your right-to-work checks, saving time and reducing risk
- Achieving process efficiencies that help minimize costs and administrative burden for employers
- Ability to perform checks consistent with the latest UK legislation
- Access to Fragomen’s wider immigration support teams when needed, to ensure employees transition seamlessly into the UK
Fragomen WorkRight is underpinned by Experian data sources and ensures checks are consistent with the latest Home Office guidelines—saving you time and giving you peace of mind that your employees genuinely have the right to work in the UK.

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
